stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She opposes the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines.
justification: In political debates, she regularly emphasises the benefits of her political positions for people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She rarely speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She opposes the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines. In political debates, she regularly emphasises the benefits of her political positions for people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
